steps.title: Read circle A's radius from its equation
steps.content: The standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$ tells us that $r_A^2 = 25$, so $r_A = 5$. The center is $(3, 9)$.

steps.title: Double the radius and square the result
steps.content: Circle B keeps the same center $(3, 9)$ but has radius $r_B = 2 \cdot 5 = 10$, so $r_B^2 = 100$. Its equation is $(x-3)^2 + (y-9)^2 = 100$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $r^2 = 625$ gives $r = 25$, which is five times circle A's radius, not twice. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $r^2 = 250$ gives $r = \sqrt{250} \approx 15.8$, not $10$. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $r^2 = 50$ gives $r = \sqrt{50} \approx 7.07$, not $10$. This would correspond to squaring the radius instead of doubling it. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $r^2 = 100$ gives $r = 10$, exactly twice circle A's radius. Matches.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
